title: Presidential Proclamation Designating a Day of Celebration in Honor of Henry Clay
summary: A presidential proclamation that designates a day of celebration to commemorate the life and historical contributions of Henry Clay on what would have been his 249th birthday. The proclamation recognizes Clay's service to the United States and his role in the formation and preservation of the Union during his lifetime of political involvement.

title: Executive Order on Federal Involvement in College Athletics Governance
summary: This executive order addresses the federal government's role in college sports administration. The order establishes that college athletics have historically provided educational and athletic opportunities to students and acknowledges the significance of collegiate sports within American culture. The order indicates federal action regarding the governance and structure of college sports systems, though the specific provisions and mechanisms for federal involvement are outlined in the subsequent sections of the order not included in this excerpt.

title: Presidential Memorandum Regarding Department of Homeland Security Operations Following Extended Funding Lapse
summary: A presidential memorandum addressed to the Secretary of Homeland Security and Director of the Office of Management and Budget regarding the resumption of operations at the Department of Homeland Security following an approximately seven-week period during which the department operated under a funding lapse. The memorandum appears to direct action to restore full operational capability to DHS personnel and functions.

title: Executive Order on Federal Contractor Compliance with Anti-Discrimination Requirements
summary: This executive order directs federal contractors to comply with existing anti-discrimination laws and regulations. The order is based on the authority granted to the President under the Constitution and federal law, including the Federal Property and Administrative Services Act. The order addresses policies and practices related to diversity, equity, and inclusion programs at federal contractors, asserting that such programs may constitute discrimination. The order establishes requirements for federal contractors regarding employment practices and organizational policies, utilizing the government's contracting authority under FPASA to enforce compliance with specified standards.

title: Continuation of the Federal Emergency Management Agency Review Council
summary: Executive order directing the continued operation of the Federal Emergency Management Agency Review Council pursuant to the Federal Advisory Committee Act. The order extends the establishment and functions of the council in accordance with chapter 10 of title 5, United States Code, and the constitutional and statutory authorities vested in the President.
